24

Artists unified on one platform

Studio documentation - Cape Town01 - 04

The Brief

An online studio that respects the craft before the software.

Tattoo Collective brought together twenty four sought-after artists, but the booking experience still depended on scattered DMs, email chains, and manually updated calendars. The work felt premium. The administration did not.

We treated the product like an extension of the studio itself: intimate, atmospheric, highly personal, and never louder than the art. Portfolio storytelling came first. Admin became invisible. Clients could finally browse, trust, and commit without friction.

The launch changed the studio rhythm immediately. Artists had clearer schedules, clients had confidence in the process, and the platform became a new part of the brand - not just a utility tucked behind it.

What We Built

01R

Research & Journey Mapping

Mapped every friction point from discovery to confirmed session across artists and clients.

02A

Artist Portfolio System

A modular showcase structure that lets each artist feel distinct without breaking cohesion.

03B

Booking Platform

Availability, deposits, and scheduling designed to feel precise, not clinical.

04C

Client Profiles

History, preferences, and references surfaced at the right moment for smoother bookings.

05I

Studio Identity

A digital expression of the studio atmosphere, rooted in craft and quiet confidence.

06M

Mobile-first Web App

Built for clients who discover an artist on social and book from their phone immediately.

Studio Atmosphere

"The platform feels like the studio sounds: calm, focused, and unmistakably ours."

How we shaped the studio online.

01
Discovery
Artist interviews, studio shadowing, and mapping every call, DM, and booking request happening behind the scenes.
02
Strategy
We framed the platform around artist craft first, with admin quietly disappearing into the background.
03
Design
Portfolio storytelling, booking flows, and availability states were refined until the product felt as precise as the artists themselves.
04
Delivery
Responsive web app, artist onboarding system, and a flexible identity that scales across studios and guest residencies.

"

Our artists were skeptical about going digital. Within a week of launch, every single one of them was fully booked through the platform. It just works.

J

Jade R.

Studio Director, Tattoo Collective
